body:
## Summary
- Rework the README opening around Sepo and self-evolving repositories
- Replace collapsed/TBD onboarding content with concrete setup, trigger, architecture, and docs sections
- Align the quick-start doc with the README and keep a full documentation index link

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `npm --prefix .agent test`

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | PR is tightly scoped to onboarding docs/content: only [README.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/93aef0d2df576e9435d16075357612e61c06d7a2/README.md), [quick-start.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/93aef0d2df576e9435d16075357612e61c06d7a2/.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md), and one new illustration asset. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README quick-start, trigger table, label routes, auth/access wording, and docs index link now match the updated quick-start structure and terminology. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | README uses goal-oriented sections (“Getting started,” “Understanding the system,” “Customizing and operating”) instead of exhaustive file-purpose inventories. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Change is documentation-only and straightforward: clearer top-level framing, concrete setup steps, route table, and architecture summary without adding extra process complexity. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | Updated text explicitly anchors to existing docs (setup guide, install-existing, access policy, overall design/docs index), indicating alignment with current documented behavior rather than introducing parallel guidance. |

## Notes

- Strongest score drivers were scope discipline and consistency: the PR directly implements the requested onboarding rewrite while keeping README and quick-start aligned.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No blocking or warning-level rubric violations found in the live PR diff for applicable rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR rewrites onboarding docs to make Sepo setup and first use concrete and immediately actionable.
- It replaces the old abstract README opening and collapsed/TBD sections with direct setup steps, trigger guidance, architecture context, and a curated docs navigation path.
- It updates both `README.md` and `.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md` in tandem, plus adds a new overview image asset.
- Reviewers validated that trigger routes, access-policy wording, and linked docs are aligned with current repository behavior.
- No parent issue/tracking-plan references were found in the PR body/comments, so review stayed scoped to PR #1 itself.

## Review
Overall judgment: this is ready to ship. Across all review inputs, no blocking or warning-level correctness issues were identified; only minor optional presentation polish was suggested by one reviewer.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable defects identified | INFO | All reviewers found the doc changes behaviorally aligned; one reviewer noted optional readability polish (PNG+Mermaid redundancy, `👀` cue wording), but not as required fixes. |

## Progress
- Existing PR discussion already includes a rubric pass indicating scope discipline and doc-sync quality.
- No inline review comments were previously present, and no new line-specific blocking/warning findings emerged that warranted posting inline feedback.
- Optional polish items were identified as non-blocking and can be deferred.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`  
No unresolved actionable branch-change work remains from the synthesized reviews.

## Final Verdict
`SHIP`

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #1.

no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #1 history and context (`gh pr view`, issue comments, PR comments, reviews, and diff), checked trust signals (`gh repo view`, collaborator lookup best-effort, `author_association`), and read all existing active rubrics under `/home/runner/work/_temp/agent-rubrics/rubrics/`.

Why no changes:
1. PR conversation contains no trusted human rubric-learning guidance beyond the PR author/merger, whose PR `author_association` is `CONTRIBUTOR`.
2. The only comments are bot-authored (`sepo-agent-app[bot]`), which are advisory and not valid alone for rubric learning.
3. No explicit trusted instruction to add/update rubrics appears in the PR conversation.
4. Themes visible in the PR (surgical scope, docs sync, read docs/context first) are already covered by existing active rubrics, so adding one would be duplicate/one-off.

title: Improve Sepo onboarding docs
